tree.title: Cloud Service Agreement
tree.summary: Cloud Service Agreement — top-level parts: Service, Restrictions & Obligations, Privacy & Security, Payment & Taxes, Term & Termination, Representations & Warranties, Disclaimer of Warranties, Limitation of Liability, Indemnification, Confidentiality, Reservation of Rights, General Terms, Definitions.

children.title: Service
children.summary: Provider delivers Technical Support per Order Form specifications and grants Customer internal use of Cloud Service and Software, while Customer maintains account security and Provider may use aggregated Usage Data and de-identified Content for service improvement and ML development.

children.title: Access and Use
children.raw: During the Subscription Period and subject to the terms of this Agreement, Customer may (a) access and use the Cloud Service; and (b) copy and use the included Software and Documentation only as needed to access and use the Cloud Service, in each case, for its internal business purposes. If a Customer Affiliate enters a separate Order Form with Provider, the Customer’s Affiliate creates a separate agreement between Provider and that Affiliate, where Provider’s responsibility to the Affiliate is individual and separate from Customer and Customer is not responsible for its Affiliates’ agreement.
children.text: During the Subscription Period and subject to the terms of this Agreement, Customer may (a) access and use the Cloud Service; and (b) copy and use the included Software and Documentation only as needed to access and use the Cloud Service, in each case, for its internal business purposes. If a Customer Affiliate enters a separate Order Form with Provider, the Customer’s Affiliate creates a separate agreement between Provider and that Affiliate, where Provider’s responsibility to the Affiliate is individual and separate from Customer and Customer is not responsible for its Affiliates’ agreement.
children.summary: Customer may access and use the Cloud Service and copy included Software and Documentation for internal business purposes during the Subscription Period; Customer Affiliates require separate Order Forms and agreements.

children.title: Support
children.raw: During the Subscription Period, Provider will provide Technical Support as described in the Order Form.
children.text: During the Subscription Period, Provider will provide Technical Support as described in the Order Form.
children.summary: Provider must deliver Technical Support during the Subscription Period according to specifications detailed in the Order Form.

children.title: User Accounts
children.raw: Customer is responsible for all actions on Users’ accounts and for all Users’ compliance with this Agreement. Customer and Users must protect the confidentiality of their passwords and login credentials. Customer will promptly notify Provider if it suspects or knows of any fraudulent activity with its accounts, passwords, or credentials, or if they become compromised.
children.text: Customer is responsible for all actions on Users’ accounts and for all Users’ compliance with this Agreement. Customer and Users must protect the confidentiality of their passwords and login credentials. Customer will promptly notify Provider if it suspects or knows of any fraudulent activity with its accounts, passwords, or credentials, or if they become compromised.
children.summary: Customer bears responsibility for all User account actions and Agreement compliance; Customer and Users must safeguard passwords/credentials and notify Provider promptly of suspected fraud or compromise.

children.title: Feedback and Usage Data
children.raw: Customer may, but is not required to, give Provider Feedback, in which case Customer gives Feedback "AS IS". Provider may use all Feedback freely without any restriction or obligation. In addition, Provider may collect and analyze Usage Data, and Provider may freely use Usage Data to maintain, improve, enhance, and promote Provider’s products and services without restriction or obligation. However, Provider may only disclose Usage Data to others if the Usage Data is aggregated and does not identify Customer or Users.

In addition, Provider may collect and analyze Usage Data, and Provider may freely use Usage Data to maintain, improve, enhance, and promote Provider’s products and services without restriction or obligation. However, Provider may only disclose Usage Data to others if the Usage Data is aggregated and does not identify Customer or Users.
children.summary: Provider may freely use Customer Feedback and collect/analyze Usage Data to maintain and improve services, but may only disclose Usage Data if aggregated and non-identifying.

children.title: Customer Content
children.raw: Provider may copy, display, modify, and use Customer Content only as needed to provide and maintain the Product and related offerings. Customer is responsible for the accuracy and content of Customer Content.
children.text: Provider may copy, display, modify, and use Customer Content only as needed to provide and maintain the Product and related offerings. Customer is responsible for the accuracy and content of Customer Content.
children.summary: Provider may copy, display, modify, and use Customer Content solely to provide and maintain the Product; Customer bears responsibility for accuracy and legality of Customer Content.

children.title: Machine Learning
children.raw: Usage Data and Customer Content may be used to develop, train, or enhance artificial intelligence or machine learning models that are part of Provider’s products and services, including third-party components of the Product, and Customer authorizes Provider to process its Usage Data and Customer Content for such purposes. However, (a) Usage Data and Customer Content must be aggregated before it can be used for these purposes, and (b) Provider will use commercially reasonable efforts consistent with industry standard technology to de-identify Usage Data and Customer Content before such use. Nothing in this section will reduce or limit Provider’s obligations regarding Personal Data that may be contained in Usage Data or Customer Content under Applicable Data Protection Laws. Due to the nature of artificial intelligence and machine learning, information generated by these features may be incorrect or inaccurate. Product features that include artificial intelligence or machine learning models are not human and are not a substitute for human oversight.

However, (a) Usage Data and Customer Content must be aggregated before it can be used for these purposes, and (b) Provider will use commercially reasonable efforts consistent with industry standard technology to de-identify Usage Data and Customer Content before such use. children.summary: Provider may use aggregated and de-identified Usage Data and Customer Content to develop AI/ML models, subject to de-identification requirements and compliance with Applicable Data Protection Laws regarding Personal Data.

children.title: Restrictions & Obligations
children.summary: Customer is prohibited from reverse engineering, sublicensing, modifying, and competitive use of the Product; Provider may suspend access for breaches of these restrictions or unpaid invoices exceeding 30 days.

children.title: Restrictions on Customer
children.raw: a. Except as expressly permitted by this Agreement, Customer will not (and will not allow anyone else to): (i) reverse engineer, decompile, or attempt to discover any source code or underlying ideas or algorithms of the Product (except to the extent Applicable Laws prohibit this restriction); (ii) provide, sell, transfer, sublicense, lend, distribute, rent, or otherwise allow others to access or use the Product; (iii) remove any proprietary notices or labels; (iv) copy, modify, or create derivative works of the Product; (v) conduct security or vulnerability tests on, interfere with the operation of, cause performance degradation of, or circumvent access restrictions of the Product; (vi) access accounts, information, data, or portions of the Product to which Customer does not have explicit authorization; (vii) use the Product to develop a competing service or product; (viii) use the Product with any High Risk Activities or with any activity prohibited by Applicable Laws; (ix) use the Product to obtain unauthorized access to anyone else’s networks or equipment; or (x) upload, submit, or otherwise make available to the Product any Customer Content to which Customer and Users do not have the proper rights. b. Use of the Product must comply with all Documentation and Use Limitations.
children.text:
a. Except as expressly permitted by this Agreement, Customer will not (and will not allow anyone else to): (i) reverse engineer, decompile, or attempt to discover any source code or underlying ideas or algorithms of the Product (except to the extent Applicable Laws prohibit this restriction); (ii) provide, sell, transfer, sublicense, lend, distribute, rent, or otherwise allow others to access or use the Product; (iii) remove any proprietary notices or labels; (iv) copy, modify, or create derivative works of the Product; (v) conduct security or vulnerability tests on, interfere with the operation of, cause performance degradation of, or circumvent access restrictions of the Product; (vi) access accounts, information, data, or portions of the Product to which Customer does not have explicit authorization; (vii) use the Product to develop a competing service or product; (viii) use the Product with any High Risk Activities or with any activity prohibited by Applicable Laws; (ix) use the Product to obtain unauthorized access to anyone else’s networks or equipment; or (x) upload, submit, or otherwise make available to the Product any Customer Content to which Customer and Users do not have the proper rights.
b. Use of the Product must comply with all Documentation and Use Limitations.
children.summary: Customer is prohibited from reverse engineering, sublicensing, modifying, security testing, unauthorized access, competitive use, high-risk activities, and uploading unlicensed content, with use subject to Documentation and Use Limitations.

children.title: Suspension
children.raw: If Customer (a) has an outstanding, undisputed balance on its account for more than 30 days; (b) breaches Section 2.1 (Restrictions on Customer); or (c) uses the Product in violation of the Agreement or in a way that materially and negatively impacts the Product or others, then Provider may temporarily suspend Customer’s access to the Product with or without notice. However, Provider will try to inform Customer before suspending Customer’s account when practical. Provider will reinstate Customer’s access to the Product only if Customer resolves the underlying issue.
children.text: If Customer (a) has an outstanding, undisputed balance on its account for more than 30 days; (b) breaches Section 2.1 (Restrictions on Customer); or (c) uses the Product in violation of the Agreement or in a way that materially and negatively impacts the Product or others, then Provider may temporarily suspend Customer’s access to the Product with or without notice. However, Provider will try to inform Customer before suspending Customer’s account when practical. Provider will reinstate Customer’s access to the Product only if Customer resolves the underlying issue.
children.summary: Provider may suspend Customer's access to the Product for unpaid invoices over 30 days, breach of Section 2.1 restrictions, or misuse; reinstatement requires resolving the underlying issue.

children.title: Privacy & Security
children.summary: Customer must execute a DPA before submitting GDPR-governed Personal Data and cannot submit Prohibited Data unless expressly authorized in Order Form or Key Terms.

children.title: Personal Data
children.raw: Before submitting Personal Data governed by GDPR, Customer must enter into a data processing agreement with Provider. If the parties have a DPA, each party will comply with its obligations in the DPA, the terms of the DPA will control each party’s rights and obligations as to Personal Data, and the terms of the DPA will control in the event of any conflict with this Agreement.
children.text: Before submitting Personal Data governed by GDPR, Customer must enter into a data processing agreement with Provider. If the parties have a DPA, each party will comply with its obligations in the DPA, the terms of the DPA will control each party’s rights and obligations as to Personal Data, and the terms of the DPA will control in the event of any conflict with this Agreement.
children.summary: Customer must execute a data processing agreement with Provider before submitting GDPR-governed Personal Data; DPA terms control rights, obligations, and conflicts regarding Personal Data.

children.title: Prohibited Data
children.raw: Customer will not (and will not allow anyone else to) submit Prohibited Data to the Product unless authorized by the Order Form or Key Terms.
children.text: Customer will not (and will not allow anyone else to) submit Prohibited Data to the Product unless authorized by the Order Form or Key Terms.
children.summary: Customer is prohibited from submitting Prohibited Data to the Product except where specifically authorized in the Order Form or Key Terms.

children.title: Payment & Taxes
children.summary: Provider bills Customer for Fees (usage-based in arrears, others in advance) in USD exclusive of taxes; Customer pays all invoiced amounts plus taxes via Payment Process, with non-refundable Fees except prorated prepaid amounts upon termination, and must dispute in good faith within 30 days.

children.title: Fees
children.raw: Unless the Order Form specifies a different currency, all Fees are in U.S. Dollars and are exclusive of taxes. Except for the prorated refund of prepaid Fees allowed with specific termination rights given in the Agreement, Fees are non-refundable.
children.text: Unless the Order Form specifies a different currency, all Fees are in U.S. Dollars and are exclusive of taxes. Except for the prorated refund of prepaid Fees allowed with specific termination rights given in the Agreement, Fees are non-refundable.
children.summary: Fees are denominated in USD, exclusive of taxes, and non-refundable except for prorated prepaid amounts upon termination under specified Agreement provisions.

children.title: Invoicing
children.raw: For a Payment Process with invoicing, Provider will send invoices for usage-based Fees in arrears and for all other Fees in advance, in each case according to the Payment Process.
children.text: For a Payment Process with invoicing, Provider will send invoices for usage-based Fees in arrears and for all other Fees in advance, in each case according to the Payment Process.
children.summary: Provider must invoice for usage-based Fees in arrears and all other Fees in advance, following the Payment Process procedures.

children.title: Automatic Payment
children.raw: For a Payment Process with automatic payment, Provider will automatically charge the credit card, debit card, or other payment method on file for Fees according to the Payment Process and Customer authorizes all such charges. In this case, Provider will make a copy of Customer’s bills or transaction history available to Customer.
children.text: For a Payment Process with automatic payment, Provider will automatically charge the credit card, debit card, or other payment method on file for Fees according to the Payment Process and Customer authorizes all such charges. In this case, Provider will make a copy of Customer’s bills or transaction history available to Customer.
children.summary: Provider automatically charges Customer's payment method on file for Fees per the Payment Process, with Customer authorization and Provider's obligation to provide billing records.

children.title: Taxes
children.raw: Customer is responsible for all duties, taxes, and levies that apply to Fees, including sales, use, VAT, GST, or withholding, that Provider itemizes and includes in an invoice. However, Customer is not responsible for Provider’s income taxes.
children.text: Customer is responsible for all duties, taxes, and levies that apply to Fees, including sales, use, VAT, GST, or withholding, that Provider itemizes and includes in an invoice. However, Customer is not responsible for Provider’s income taxes.
children.summary: Customer must pay all duties, taxes, and levies on Fees that Provider invoices, excluding Provider's income taxes.

children.title: Payment
children.raw: Customer will pay Provider Fees and taxes in U.S. Dollars, unless the Order Form specifies a different currency, according to the Payment Process.
children.text: Customer will pay Provider Fees and taxes in U.S. Dollars, unless the Order Form specifies a different currency, according to the Payment Process.
children.summary: Customer must pay Provider Fees and applicable taxes in U.S. Dollars (or alternate currency if specified in Order Form) using the Payment Process.

children.title: Payment Dispute
children.raw: If Customer has a good-faith disagreement about the Fees charged or invoiced, Customer must notify Provider about the dispute before payment is due, or within 30 days of an automatic payment, and must pay all undisputed amounts on time. The parties will work together to resolve the dispute within 15 days. If no resolution is agreed, each party may pursue any remedies available under the Agreement or Applicable Laws.
children.text: If Customer has a good-faith disagreement about the Fees charged or invoiced, Customer must notify Provider about the dispute before payment is due, or within 30 days of an automatic payment, and must pay all undisputed amounts on time. The parties will work together to resolve the dispute within 15 days. If no resolution is agreed, each party may pursue any remedies available under the Agreement or Applicable Laws.
children.summary: Customer must notify Provider of good-faith fee disputes before payment due or within 30 days post-automatic payment, pay undisputed amounts timely, and the parties will attempt resolution within 15 days before pursuing other remedies.

children.title: Term & Termination
children.summary: This clause establishes automatic renewal of Subscription Periods with opt-out rights, immediate termination rights for material breach or insolvency, Force Majeure termination with prorated refunds, and cascading termination effects including Product access loss and Content deletion within 60 days.

children.title: Order Form and Agreement
children.raw: For each Order Form, the Agreement will start on the Order Date, continue through the Subscription Period, and automatically renew for additional Subscription Periods unless one party gives notice of non-renewal to the other party before the Non-Renewal Notice Date.
children.text: For each Order Form, the Agreement will start on the Order Date, continue through the Subscription Period, and automatically renew for additional Subscription Periods unless one party gives notice of non-renewal to the other party before the Non-Renewal Notice Date.
children.summary: Agreement term commences on Order Date, runs through each Subscription Period, and auto-renews unless either party provides non-renewal notice before the Non-Renewal Notice Date.

children.title: Framework Terms
children.raw: These Framework Terms will start on the Effective Date and continue for the longer of one year or until all Order Forms governed by the Framework Terms have ended.
children.text: These Framework Terms will start on the Effective Date and continue for the longer of one year or until all Order Forms governed by the Framework Terms have ended.
children.summary: Framework Terms commence on the Effective Date and continue for the longer of one year or until all governed Order Forms terminate.

children.title: Termination
children.summary: Either party may immediately terminate the Framework Terms or Order Form for material breach (with 30-day cure period) or insolvency/dissolution of the other party.

children.title: Force Majeure
children.raw: Either party may terminate an affected Order Form upon notice if a Force Majeure Event prevents the Product from materially operating for 30 or more consecutive days. Provider will pay to Customer a prorated refund of any prepaid Fees for the remainder of the Subscription Period. A Force Majeure Event does not excuse Customer’s obligation to pay Fees accrued prior to termination.
children.text: Either party may terminate an affected Order Form upon notice if a Force Majeure Event prevents the Product from materially operating for 30 or more consecutive days. Provider will pay to Customer a prorated refund of any prepaid Fees for the remainder of the Subscription Period. A Force Majeure Event does not excuse Customer’s obligation to pay Fees accrued prior to termination.
children.summary: Either party may terminate an Order Form if Force Majeure prevents the Product from materially operating 30+ consecutive days; Provider refunds prepaid Fees prorated to termination, but Customer remains liable for accrued Fees.

children.title: Effect of Termination
children.raw: Termination of the Framework Terms will automatically terminate all Order Forms governed by the Framework Terms. Upon any expiration or termination: a. Customer will no longer have any right to use the Product. b. Upon Customer’s request, Provider will delete Customer Content within 60 days. c. Each Recipient will return or destroy Discloser’s Confidential Information in its possession or control. d. Provider will submit a final bill or invoice for all outstanding Fees accrued before termination and Customer will pay the invoice according to Section 4 (Payment & Taxes).
children.text:
Termination of the Framework Terms will automatically terminate all Order Forms governed by the Framework Terms. Upon any expiration or termination:
a. Customer will no longer have any right to use the Product.
b. Upon Customer’s request, Provider will delete Customer Content within 60 days.
c. Each Recipient will return or destroy Discloser’s Confidential Information in its possession or control.
d. Provider will submit a final bill or invoice for all outstanding Fees accrued before termination and Customer will pay the invoice according to Section 4 (Payment & Taxes).
children.summary: Upon termination of Framework Terms, all Order Forms terminate automatically; Customer loses Product access, Provider deletes Customer Content within 60 days upon request, Recipients return/destroy Confidential Information, and Provider bills Customer for accrued Fees per Section 4.

children.title: Survival
children.raw: a. The following sections will survive expiration or termination of the Agreement: Section 1.4 (Feedback and Usage Data), Section 1.6 (Machine Learning), Section 2.1 (Restrictions on Customer), Section 4 (Payment & Taxes) for Fees accrued or payable before expiration or termination, Section 5.5 (Effect of Termination), Section 5.6 (Survival), Section 6 (Representations & Warranties), Section 7 (Disclaimer of Warranties), Section 8 (Limitation of Liability), Section 9 (Indemnification), Section 10 (Confidentiality), Section 11 (Reservation of Rights), Section 12 (General Terms), Section 13 (Definitions), and the portions of a Cover Page referenced by these sections. b. Each Recipient may retain Discloser’s Confidential Information in accordance with its standard backup or record retention policies maintained in the ordinary course of business or as required by Applicable Laws, in which case Section 3 (Privacy & Security) and Section 10 (Confidentiality) will continue to apply to retained Confidential Information.
children.text:
a. The following sections will survive expiration or termination of the Agreement: Section 1.4 (Feedback and Usage Data), Section 1.6 (Machine Learning), Section 2.1 (Restrictions on Customer), Section 4 (Payment & Taxes) for Fees accrued or payable before expiration or termination, Section 5.5 (Effect of Termination), Section 5.6 (Survival), Section 6 (Representations & Warranties), Section 7 (Disclaimer of Warranties), Section 8 (Limitation of Liability), Section 9 (Indemnification), Section 10 (Confidentiality), Section 11 (Reservation of Rights), Section 12 (General Terms), Section 13 (Definitions), and the portions of a Cover Page referenced by these sections.
b. Each Recipient may retain Discloser’s Confidential Information in accordance with its standard backup or record retention policies maintained in the ordinary course of business or as required by Applicable Laws, in which case Section 3 (Privacy & Security) and Section 10 (Confidentiality) will continue to apply to retained Confidential Information.
children.summary: Specifies which Agreement sections survive termination and permits Recipients to retain Confidential Information per backup/retention policies while maintaining confidentiality obligations.

children.title: Representations & Warranties
children.summary: Each party represents legal authority and organizational standing; Customer warrants User rights to submit content; Provider warrants Cloud Service functionality won't materially reduce and must restore it within 45 days or face termination and prorated refund.

children.title: Mutual
children.raw: Each party represents and warrants to the other that: (a) it has the legal power and authority to enter into this Agreement; (b) it is duly organized, validly existing, and in good standing under the Applicable Laws of the jurisdiction of its origin; (c) it will comply with all Applicable Laws in performing its obligations or exercising its rights in this Agreement; and (d) it will comply with the Additional Warranties.

children.summary: Each party represents and warrants its legal authority to enter the Agreement, valid organizational status, compliance with Applicable Laws, and adherence to Additional Warranties.

children.title: From Customer
children.raw: Customer represents and warrants that it, all Users, and anyone submitting Customer Content each have and will continue to have all rights necessary to submit or make available Customer Content to the Product and to allow the use of Customer Content as described in the Agreement.
children.text: Customer represents and warrants that it, all Users, and anyone submitting Customer Content each have and will continue to have all rights necessary to submit or make available Customer Content to the Product and to allow the use of Customer Content as described in the Agreement.
children.summary: Customer warrants that it and all Users possess all necessary rights to submit Customer Content to the Product and permit its use as described in the Agreement.

children.title: From Provider
children.raw: Provider represents and warrants to Customer that it will not materially reduce the general functionality of the Cloud Service during the Subscription Period.
children.text: Provider represents and warrants to Customer that it will not materially reduce the general functionality of the Cloud Service during the Subscription Period.
children.summary: Provider warrants it will not materially reduce Cloud Service general functionality during the Subscription Period.

children.title: Provider Warranty Remedy
children.raw: If Provider breaches the warranty in Section 6.3 (Representations & Warranties from Provider), Customer must give Provider notice (with enough detail for Provider to understand or replicate the issue) within 45 days of discovering the issue. Within 45 days of receiving sufficient details of the warranty issue, Provider will attempt to restore the general functionality of the Cloud Service. If Provider cannot resolve the issue, Customer may terminate the affected Order Form and Provider will pay to Customer a prorated refund of prepaid Fees for the remainder of the Subscription Period. Provider’s restoration obligation, and Customer’s termination right, are Customer’s only remedies if Provider does not meet the warranty in Section 6.3 (Representations & Warranties from Provider).

Within 45 days of receiving sufficient details of the warranty issue, Provider will attempt to restore the general functionality of the Cloud Service. If Provider cannot resolve the issue, Customer may terminate the affected Order Form and Provider will pay to Customer a prorated refund of prepaid Fees for the remainder of the Subscription Period. Provider’s restoration obligation, and Customer’s termination right, are Customer’s only remedies if Provider does not meet the warranty in Section 6.3 (Representations & Warranties from Provider).
children.summary: Provider must restore Cloud Service functionality within 45 days of receiving notice of a Section 6.3 warranty breach, or Customer may terminate the affected Order Form and receive a prorated refund—these remedies are exclusive.

children.title: Disclaimer of Warranties
children.summary: Provider disclaims all implied warranties except express warranties in Section 6, excluding liability for merchantability, fitness, misuse, unauthorized modifications, and third-party products.

children.raw: Provider makes no guarantees that the Product will always be safe, secure, or error-free, or that it will function without disruptions, delays, or imperfections. The warranties in Section 6 (Representations & Warranties) do not apply to any misuse or unauthorized modification of the Product, nor to any product or service provided by anyone other than Provider. Except for the warranties in Section 6 (Representations & Warranties), Provider and Customer each **disclaim all other warranties and conditions, whether express or implied, including the implied warranties and conditions of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, title, and non-infringement. These disclaimers apply to the maximum extent permitted by Applicable Laws**.

children.title: Limitation of Liability
children.summary: Both parties' liability is capped at General Cap Amount (or Increased Cap Amount for Increased Claims) and damages for lost profits, consequential, and punitive damages are waived, except for Unlimited Claims and Confidentiality breaches.

children.title: Liability Caps
children.raw: a. **Except as provided in Section 8.4 (Exceptions), each party’s total cumulative liability for all claims arising out of or relating to this Agreement will not be more than the General Cap Amount.** b. **If there are Increased Claims, each party’s total cumulative liability for all Increased Claims arising out of or relating to this Agreement will not be more than the Increased Cap Amount.**
children.text:
a. Except as provided in Section 8.4 (Exceptions), each party’s total cumulative liability for all claims arising out of or relating to this Agreement will not be more than the General Cap Amount.
b. If there are Increased Claims, each party’s total cumulative liability for all Increased Claims arising out of or relating to this Agreement will not be more than the Increased Cap Amount.
children.summary: Each party's liability for all Agreement-related claims is capped at the General Cap Amount, or the Increased Cap Amount if Increased Claims occur, except as provided in Section 8.4.

children.title: Damages Waiver
children.raw: **Except as provided in Section 8.4 (Exceptions), under no circumstances will either party be liable to the other for lost profits or revenues (whether direct or indirect), or for consequential, special, indirect, exemplary, punitive, or incidental damages relating to this Agreement, even if the party is informed of the possibility of this type of damage in advance.**

children.summary: Both parties waive claims for lost profits, consequential, special, indirect, exemplary, punitive, and incidental damages under this Agreement, subject to exceptions in Section 8.4.

children.title: Applicability
children.raw: **The limitations and waivers contained in Sections 8.1 (Liability Caps) and 8.2 (Damages Waiver) apply to all liability, whether in tort (including negligence), contract, breach of statutory duty, or otherwise.**
children.text: The limitations and waivers contained in Sections 8.1 (Liability Caps) and 8.2 (Damages Waiver) apply to all liability, whether in tort (including negligence), contract, breach of statutory duty, or otherwise.
children.summary: Section 8.3 clarifies that the liability caps and damages waivers in §§ 8.1–8.2 apply broadly to all liability claims regardless of legal theory.

children.title: Exceptions
children.raw: The liability cap in Section 8.1(a) does not apply to any Increased Claims. Section 8.1 (Liability Caps) does not apply to any Unlimited Claims. Section 8.2 (Damages Waiver) does not apply to any Increased Claims or a breach of Section 10 (Confidentiality). Nothing in this Agreement will limit, exclude, or restrict a party's liability to the extent prohibited by Applicable Laws.

Section 8.2 (Damages Waiver) does not apply to any Increased Claims or a breach of Section 10 (Confidentiality). Nothing in this Agreement will limit, exclude, or restrict a party's liability to the extent prohibited by Applicable Laws.
children.summary: Section 8.1(a) liability caps exclude Increased Claims; Section 8.1 excludes Unlimited Claims; Section 8.2 damages waiver excludes Increased Claims and Section 10 Confidentiality breaches; statutory liability limits are preserved.

children.title: Indemnification
children.summary: Provider indemnifies Customer against third-party Provider Covered Claims while Customer indemnifies Provider against third-party Customer Covered Claims, each subject to prompt notice, cooperation, sole defense control, and exclusions for unauthorized modifications or misuse under §§ 9.1–9.5.

children.title: Protection by Provider
children.raw: Provider will indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Customer from and against all Provider Covered Claims made by someone other than Customer, Customer’s Affiliates, or Users, and all out-of-pocket damages, awards, settlements, costs, and expenses, including reasonable attorneys’ fees and other legal expenses, that arise from the Provider Covered Claims.
children.text: Provider will indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Customer from and against all Provider Covered Claims made by someone other than Customer, Customer’s Affiliates, or Users, and all out-of-pocket damages, awards, settlements, costs, and expenses, including reasonable attorneys’ fees and other legal expenses, that arise from the Provider Covered Claims.
children.summary: Provider indemnifies Customer against third-party Provider Covered Claims and associated damages, excluding claims by Customer, its Affiliates, or Users.

children.title: Protection by Customer
children.raw: Customer will indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Provider from and against all Customer Covered Claims made by someone other than Provider or its Affiliates, and all out-of-pocket damages, awards, settlements, costs, and expenses, including reasonable attorneys’ fees and other legal expenses, that arise from the Customer Covered Claims.

children.title: Procedure
children.raw: The Indemnifying Party’s obligations in this section are contingent upon the Protected Party: (a) promptly notifying the Indemnifying Party of each Covered Claim for which it seeks protection; (b) providing reasonable assistance to the Indemnifying Party at the Indemnifying Party’s expense; and (c) giving the Indemnifying Party sole control over the defense and settlement of each Covered Claim. A Protected Party may participate in a Covered Claim for which it seeks protection with its own attorneys only at its own expense. The Indemnifying Party may not agree to any settlement of a Covered Claim that contains an admission of fault or otherwise materially and adversely impacts the Protected Party without the prior written consent of the Protected Party.
